rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE PORTSIDE 503 TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petitioner Sharon Browne Nassiri?S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Preliminary Injunction
rulings.Rulings: [2 of 2] Nassiri alleges that if a preliminary injunction is not issued, Thompson will continue to transfer assets from the Trust with impunity until the Trust is fully exhausted; however, the decedent died over four years ago, but the record before the court indicates that Thompson has not fully exhausted the Trust. While Nassiri has presented evidence that Thompson may have breached her fiduciary duties by failing to fund Trust 1 and Trust 2 on the decedent's death, Nassiri has not met her burden to show that she would suffer irreparable harm if this injunction is not issued. Furthermore, Nassiri is requesting that Thompson be enjoined from transferring any Trust assets (be they currently held in the name of the Trust or previously held in the name of the Trust). If Thompson does not have assets other than those now held or previously held in the name of the Trust, this could leave Thompson with no funds with which to live. The Court must exercise its discretion "in favor of the party most likely to be injured." Robbins v. Sup. Ct. (County of Sacramento) (1985) 38 Cal.3d 199, 211. In the balancing test required for this equitable relief, the Court finds that Thompson will suffer the greater harm if the injunction is issued. For the foregoing reasons Nassiri's Motion for a Preliminary Injunction is DENIED. Respondent Rosie Thompson shall lodge with the clerk in Department 204 by the time set for this hearing a proposed order consistent with this tentative. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on the day before the hearing. Remote appearance is available in conformity with the current General Order of the Probate and Mental Health Division, available on the court's website at https://sfsuperiorcourt.org/divisions/probate. To appear by Zoom, go to zoom.us and click "Join Meeting." Enter Meeting ID number 87580177925 and passcode 722809. To appear telephonically through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts. Enter Meeting ID number 87580177925 and passcode 722809. Parties appearing via Zoom should use their full names in the "Your Name" section. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time in Department 204.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE PORTSIDE 503 TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Motion For Preliminary Injunction (Added For Posting Of The Tentative Ruling)
rulings.Rulings: [1 of 2] This tentative ruling is issued by Judge Ross C. Moody. Now before the Court is Petitioner Sharon Browne Nassiri's Motion for Preliminary Injunction. The purpose of a preliminary injunction is to preserve the status quo and prevent irreparable harm pending trial on the merits. See White v. Davis (2003) 30 Cal.4th 528, 554; Costa Mesa City Employees' Ass'n v. City of Costa Mesa (2012) 209 Ca.App.4th 298, 305. "A superior court must evaluate two interrelated factors when ruling on a request for a preliminary injunction: (1) the likelihood that the plaintiff will prevail on the merits at trial and (2) the interim harm that the plaintiff would be likely to sustain if the injunction were denied as compared to the harm the defendant would be likely to suffer if the preliminary injunction were issued." Smith v. Adventist Health System/West (2010) 182 Cal.App.4th 729, 749. Nassiri has alleged: (1) that the Portside 503 Trust (the "Trust") had approximately $6 million in assets on the decedent's death; (2) that at the decedent's death the Trust should have been distributed equally (approximately $3 million each) to two subtrusts - Trust 1 (essentially a decedent's trust) and Trust 2 (essentially a survivor's trust); (3) that Thompson is entitled to the assets of Trust 1 for her heath needs and maintenance during her lifetime if Trust 2 is exhausted; (4) that at Thompson's death Nassiri is to receive 50% of the residue of Trust 1; and (5) that Thompson has transferred approximately $1.5 million from the Trust (approximately 25% of the Trust) to herself and her family members. Nassiri has not disputed that Thompson is entitled, without restriction, to the 50% of the Trust that Nassiri alleges was to be distributed to Trust 2. Even accepting all of Nassiri's allegations as true, it appears that the Trust still holds 75% of the total assets it held at the decedent's death; Nassiri alleges she is to receive up to 25% of the total assets at Thompson's death. Even if Nassiri has demonstrated a reasonable probability of succeeding on the merits of her underlying petition, she does not have a reasonable probability of obtaining a judgment in excess of $1.5 million, but she has requested that the Court enjoin Thompson from transferring approximately $6 million in assets.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF FRANCES JOSEPHINE THOMPSON
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/21/22 to be heard after the heirship petition. It is unclear why the administrator has listed and given notice to paternal cousins (and more remote) if "the paternal line has completely died out", and Eugene B. Thompson, who was listed on Attachment 8 to the amended petition filed on 6/10/20 as the son of David Thompson (address unknown) was not listed on Exhibit C to this petition or given notice (and notice to him does not appear to have been dispensed with). (There is no LRSF 14.88. The petition prayer has the bond fee and the partial attorney fee reversed.) Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 3/11/22. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CAROLE STRAUSS
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION TO TERMINATE ESTATE (NO ASSETS)
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing, although the disclosure regarding Probate Code Section 9202(b) was incomplete. Notice to the CDVCB is required if the general personal representative or estate attorney knows that an heir OR BENEFICIARY is or has previously been confined in a prison or facility under the jurisdiction of the Department of Corrections and Rehabilitation or confined in any county or city jail, road camp, industrial farm, or other local correctional facility. [Emphasis added.]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF AIDA QUEBRAL ORQUIZA
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/7/22. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: No proof of publication has been filed. [Probate Code Sect. 8120.] No Acknowledgment of Receipt of the Duties and Liabilities or Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License has been filed. [LRSF 14.18 E.] The petition has many erroneous boxes checked, suggesting that the decedent had a will (see items 3e(1) and 3f(3) and 3g(1)(b) and (d)). Item 5a(7) or (8) of the petition must be answered. If the decedent's siblings survived, item 6b of the petition should have been checked. Item 7 of the petition is also incomplete. When second generation or more distant heirs are listed, the deceased ancestor(s) through whom they take (or would have taken) must be named, the relationship(s) to the decedent stated, and the date(s) of death specified. [LRSF 14.18 C.3.] Whose children are the nieces and nephews listed in item 8 of the petition? Who were the decedent's parents and when did they die? Who was the decedent's spouse and when did he die? The petition shows the name of the decedent as Aida Quebral Orquiza, but the proof of service shows the name on the face of the Notice of Petition to Administer Estate as "Aida Qrquza Quebral". Also, the proof of service shows that the petitioner, who IS an interested party, served the notice. (The Notice of Petition to Administer Estate also refers to a will.) This Court does not generally allow blocked accounts at the outset of the estate. [LRSF 14.16 M.] It appears that an AMENDED PETITION must be filed to address all of these issues. Notice of the AMENDED PETITION must be mailed (by a disinterested party) and also published after it is filed. Courtesy copies of the AMENDED PETITION and any supporting papers, including proof of service and proof of publication of the AMENDED PETITION, must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 2/24/22. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.
